summary:
The “needle” was in the InstallSnapshot handler.  
It forgot to:

1. Advance commitIndex to at least the snapshot’s last-included index.
2. Compact the local log so the covered entries aren’t replayed later.

Because commitIndex stayed behind (often at 0), a restart caused
Restart() to believe nothing was committed after the snapshot and it
re-applied the same entries, yielding duplicates.

Fix (snapshot.go):

```
	// Ensure commitIndex includes the snapshot
	if snap.LastIncludedIndex > n.commitIndex {
		n.commitIndex = snap.LastIncludedIndex
	}

	// Drop log entries now covered by the snapshot
	n.raftLog.CompactUpTo(snap.LastIncludedIndex)
```

(plus existing state-machine restore & lastApplied update).

After patching, running test.sh shows:

```
PASS: No duplicate state machine transitions across all rounds
```

Problem solved.
